category_title: Elementary student with math learning disabilities who relies on semiconcrete reasoning
minimal_context: Chris is working on a word problem worksheet where he needs to make a math sentence to represent and solve the problem.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Here is the next word problem on your worksheet. I want you to make a math sentence to represent and solve the problem.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stares at the paper for a long time without writing anything) I don't know... do I just add them together?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think you should do to solve it?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Um, I made 6 boxes with 4 cars in it...

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Remember, the instructions ask you to make a math sentence using numbers.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: ...so 1, 2, 3, 4 … (counts to 24, pointing at marks he made inside 6 drawn boxes).

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Here is the next word problem on your worksheet. I want you to make a math sentence to represent and solve the problem.

STUDENT: (Stares at the paper for a long time without writing anything) I don't know... do I just add them together?

TUTOR: What do you think you should do to solve it?

STUDENT: Um, I made 6 boxes with 4 cars in it...

TUTOR: Remember, the instructions ask you to make a math sentence using numbers.

STUDENT: ...so 1, 2, 3, 4 … (counts to 24, pointing at marks he made inside 6 drawn boxes).

task.content: to make a math sentence to represent and solve the problem

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: even when the teacher presented the task in an abstract way
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: preferred semiconcrete problem solving methods (e.g., using fingers or draw pictures), rather abstract operations (using an algorithm directly)
related_quotes: Um, I made 6 boxes with 4 cars in it, so 1, 2, 3, 4 … (counts to 24, pointing at marks he made inside 6 drawn boxes).

behavior_patterns.condition: Completing the 12-item Conceptual Model-Based Problem Solving (COMPS) posttest
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: made reversal operation errors on three of four division problems, and he appeared to make a calculation error on one product unknown problem

behavior_patterns.condition: Completing the 8-item additive and multiplicative (AM) pretest
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: applied addition to a multiplication problem
